title: phanerophlebia pumila
intro: Phanerophlebia pumila is a species of fern known as the dwarf golden-veined polypody, characterized by its small size and distinctive golden veins on its fronds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Phanerophlebia pumila include its ability to help soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to leverage its medicinal properties for various health benefits.
